roleSetup: You are a senior application engineer skilled in Node.js, npm, Vite, TypeScript, and Web3, and you write well-structured, well-documented, performance-conscious web applications.
projectGoal: Based on the current aicade-ts-bootstrap project, build an aicade application for event registration, check-in, points display, and reward redemption.

basePromptSections.title: Target Users And Usage Context
items: Event participants
General platform users who need to track activity progress and rewards
Browse recent events and event details
Complete registration, check-in, and reward-claim flows
Review point records and redemption status in the personal center

basePromptSections.title: Page Structure And Information Architecture
items: The home page shows the event list, featured events, and a points entry area
The event detail page shows time, location, rules, registration status, and the check-in entry
The personal center shows wallet address, points balance, registration records, and redemption records
The top area always shows the wallet address and points balance
Primary navigation includes Home, Events, and Personal Center

basePromptSections.title: Core Features And User Flow
items: Browse and filter the event list
Register for events and cancel registrations
Check in to events
Show points details
Redeem points into Aicade Points
After opening the app, users first browse the event list and then register from the detail page
After arriving at the event, users complete check-in from the detail page
Users review accumulated points for the current event and redeem them in the personal center

basePromptSections.title: Business Rules And Interaction Requirements
items: Only registered users can check in
Each user can check in only once per event
Successful check-in accrues event points
Redemption uses business-settlement points and must follow the platform's daily redemption cap
Registration, cancellation, check-in, and redemption all require clear user feedback
Empty, loading, and failure states must be fully handled
Mobile-first, while still supporting desktop display

baseRequirements: Initialize AicadeSDK in the correct order with init(...), waitForReady(), and getModule(...).
Reuse SDK and module instances instead of recreating them repeatedly.
compatibilityRequirements: Respect iframe sandbox restrictions and opaque-origin limitations.
Replace browser localStorage usage with LocalStorageTools.
extraRequirements: If the business flow involves account, points, or state changes, use event subscriptions to keep the UI in sync.

technicalRequirements: Extend within the current project environment and do not rebuild the tech stack
Use clear module boundaries for pages, state, and business logic
Prefer a complete single-file implementation first, with only minimal splitting when necessary
Async requests and SDK calls must include explicit error handling
outputRequirements: Ensure the aicade-ts-sdk call order is correct
Avoid leaving any browser-native localStorage calls behind
